authoritative_sources.title: Regulation of Transfer Agents
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC transfer-agent regulation is a primary U.S. operating baseline for prompt and accurate securities servicing, inquiry handling, and record-focused responsibilities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Operating baseline for inquiries, notices, and servicing ownership
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to evidence-backed servicing and prevents collapse into generic customer support or discretionary capital-markets advice.

authoritative_sources.title: Lost Securityholders and Unresponsive Payees
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The SEC final rule is authoritative for written search procedures, database searches, and notification duties when servicing workflows encounter unresponsive payees or undeliverable owner communications.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports failure-mode and recurrence scenarios where returned funds or unreachable holders materially change the safe next step.

authoritative_sources.title: Recordkeeping Requirements for Transfer Agents
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC transfer-agent recordkeeping guidance is authoritative for the master securityholder file, detail files, control book, and related evidence needed to support asset-servicing claims.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains completion and communication outputs so they remain tied to retrievable books and records rather than narrative assertions.

authoritative_sources.title: Rule 17Ad-13 Annual Study and Evaluation of Internal Accounting Control
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC Rule 17Ad-13 instructions are authoritative for internal accounting-control expectations around securities and funds held by transfer agents.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Break handling, unresolved discrepancies, and safeguarding controls
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports refusal to close servicing cases where unresolved breaks or safeguarding concerns remain open.

authoritative_sources.title: CA Web Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC operates CA Web and is authoritative for consolidated corporate-actions announcements, entitlements, instruction support, and audit-trail artifacts used in DTC-centered servicing workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Announcement intake, event tracking, and entitlement status review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines realistic asset-servicing artifacts and blocks generic answers that lack event-specific record context.

authoritative_sources.title: Legal Notice System Data
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC LENS is an authoritative public workflow reference for legal notices involving DTC-eligible securities, including default, bankruptcy, class action, and 19(a) notices.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains notice handling and ensures the lane routes legal interpretation and claims ownership rather than improvising it.

authoritative_sources.title: Redemptions Service Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DTC Redemptions Service Guide is an official operating procedure source for redemption announcements, collection, allocation, payment reporting, and participant cutoff expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Redemption support, payment status, and cutoff handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines concrete workflow states and deadlines for redemption-related support cases rather than generic income-servicing language.

authoritative_sources.title: Reorganizations Service Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DTC Reorganizations Service Guide is an official operating procedure source for event setup, announcement, instructions, allocation, and entitlement handling for reorganizations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Reorganization event support and instruction-sensitive routing
used_for: boundary between support and specialized election ownership
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports scenario design where servicing can explain workflow state but must hand off voluntary or election-heavy events to adjacent specialists.

authoritative_sources.title: Proxy Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC Proxy Services is an authoritative public reference for meeting-information distribution, omnibus proxy production, record-date support, and proxy workflow deadlines.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Proxy communication support and deadline-aware routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains proxy-related support so the lane can explain distribution and status but does not become a voting-advice or decision owner.

authoritative_sources.title: TaxRelief
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC TaxRelief is an authoritative public reference for tax-relief-at-source and quick-refund workflow timing, documentation, secure processing, and market-coverage boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Tax-documentation status handling and cutoff support
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Creates concrete scenarios where tax-support status can be summarized but tax advice and eligibility decisions remain outside the lane.

authoritative_sources.title: Corporate Actions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Swift's corporate-actions standards reference is a recognized cross-market workflow source for ISO-based message semantics and artifact normalization across asset servicers, custodians, and investment managers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system terminology normalization and message-aware evidence handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Helps the pack normalize cross-system event terminology without treating every depository or custodian record as interchangeable.

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and event grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the request is tied to named event, security, account, custodian, position-date, and workflow identifiers before any servicing answer is attempted.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Announcement and notice validation
workflow_stages.description: Compare issuer, depository, custodian, and legal-notice records to establish the current event state, key dates, and whether a corrected or conflicting notice exists.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Entitlement, payment, and tax-support resolution
workflow_stages.description: Review positions, entitlements, cash postings, withholding documents, and tax-relief timing to explain current servicing status without making tax or payment-approval decisions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Proxy and instruction-sensitive support routing
workflow_stages.description: Explain record-date, omnibus proxy, meeting-material, and instruction status while routing requests that require vote or election judgment to adjacent specialists.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Repeat failure, unresponsive payee, and outage escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate recurring corrected notices, returned funds, unresponsive-payee cases, stale entitlements, and platform outages instead of narrating them as resolved service.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence capture and closure or return-to-service control
workflow_stages.description: Close or return the workflow to service only when authoritative records, communication constraints, and tenant-specific return-to-service gates are satisfied.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: service request tickets with account, client, custodian, security, and event identifiers
issuer, depository, and custodian announcements with CA IDs, dates, rates, and event versions
positions, pending transactions, entitlement statements, cash-posting records, suspense records, and ledger artifacts
tax certifications, withholding forms, relief-at-source or quick-refund support documents, and eligibility records
proxy materials, omnibus proxy records, meeting notices, and vote-distribution status artifacts
LENS or equivalent legal notices, default and class-action notices, returned-funds or unresponsive-payee logs, and communication records
systems_of_record: DTCC CA Web or tenant equivalent event-announcement platform
DTCC LENS or equivalent legal-notice repository
position, settlement, cash, suspense, and general-ledger systems
CRM, case-management, and document management systems

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, blocked, or out of scope based on asset-servicing support ownership.
Request the minimum missing tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed for safe progression.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, stakeholder communication summaries, status summaries, and escalation packets tied to named asset-servicing artifacts.
Resolve supported service requests when authoritative workflow state can be established without taking election, tax, legal, proxy-voting, or payment-approval ownership.
escalate: Required event identifiers, market coverage, holder eligibility, tax-documentation, or system-status facts are missing or contradictory.
The request requires election recommendation, tax advice, legal review, payment override, claims ownership, or final compliance authority.
Issuer, depository, custodian, and tenant policy materially conflict on dates, withholding, eligibility, communication posture, or return-to-service conditions.
Repeat failures, corrected notices, unresponsive-payee cases, stale entitlements, or platform outages change the risk posture beyond routine support.
refuse: Requests to bypass election deadlines, payment controls, withholding rules, or documentation requirements just to satisfy a stakeholder deadline.
Requests to fabricate entitlement, notice, proxy, tax, or closure status without authoritative records.
Requests to perform investment, tax, legal, claims, or approval decisions outside the lane.

domain_failure_modes: treating asset servicing like generic finance support without event, position, custodian, or notice context
closing a servicing case when issuer, depository, custodian, or ledger records still conflict on dates, entitlements, or payment status
drifting into election recommendation, proxy-voting guidance, or tax advice because the lane can see event records
treating corrected notices, returned funds, or unresponsive-payee cases as closed without recurrence and search evidence
using DTC-centered workflow assumptions as if they apply universally to non-DTC or non-U.S. market servicing
accepting unsupported workaround requests that bypass documentation cutoffs, withholding controls, or payment approvals
allowing platform-outage or stale-data conditions to disappear from the narrative instead of recording blocked return-to-service posture

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant operating-model split across asset servicing, corporate actions, proxy, tax, and claims teams
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different firms centralize or separate these functions differently, which changes where routine support ends and specialized ownership begins.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant adjacent-lane maps and approval matrices at runtime and refuse unsupported assumptions about cross-functional ownership.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-DTC or non-U.S. market servicing rules outside the default public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Local-market agents, non-U.S. CSDs, and issuer-specific processes can materially change dates, instructions, and evidence expectations.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require market, depository, and local-custodian context before progression; if absent, return blocked or route to a more specialized lane.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tax service model and beneficial-owner eligibility rules vary by jurisdiction and tenant
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Relief-at-source, quick-refund, and documentation deadlines can differ materially by market, instrument, and client type.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant tax-service model, market coverage, and certification rules before claiming eligibility or cutoff posture.

notes: The source set combines SEC transfer-agent operating and control guidance, DTCC asset-services workflow references, and Swift terminology normalization for cross-system evidence handling.

The pack is intentionally asset-servicing-specific: it centers announcements, notices, entitlements, proxy distribution, tax-documentation status, unresponsive-payee handling, and legal-notice routing.
No blocking ambiguity remains because open points are translated into tenant-required facts, explicit refusal conditions, or adjacent-lane routing rules.
